questions.subject: Economy
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the primary rationale behind a country imposing an 'anti-dumping duty' on foreign imports?

options.text: To offset the impact of direct export subsidies provided by a foreign government to its manufacturers.
To act as a protectionist tariff against foreign goods exported at a price significantly lower than their domestic market price.
To penalize foreign countries that deliberately devalue their currency to make their exports cheaper.
To generate additional tax revenue from imported luxury goods in order to reduce the fiscal deficit.

questions.explanation: An anti-dumping duty is specifically designed as a protectionist tariff imposed on foreign imports that are 'dumped' in the domestic market. Dumping is the practice of exporting a product at a significantly lower price than its domestic market price. Options A, C, and D describe countervailing duties, currency manipulation countermeasures, and revenue tariffs respectively.
questions.takeaway: Anti-dumping duties are distinct from countervailing duties (which target subsidies) and are purely aimed at countering the practice of companies exporting goods at prices lower than their home market rates.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: An anti-dumping duty is a protectionist tariff imposed on foreign imports dumped in the domestic market. Dumping occurs when a company exports a product at a significantly lower price than its domestic market price.
